question: What does a yellow flag with red stripes indicate in Formula 1?
last_answer: The yellow flag with red stripes, also known as the striped flag, indicates the existence on the track of an element that causes a reduction in grip. [Source: The yellow flag with red stripes, also known as the striped flag, indicates the existence on the track of an element that causes a reduction in grip.] It can be shown due to oil residues or the presence of car fragments on the track, it is also shown in those areas of the circuit where the track is dry and it is beginning to rain. question: What was the greatest technological advance introduced by Lotus in 1962?
last_answer: In 1962, Lotus introduced a car with an aluminium monocoque chassis instead of the traditional tubular frame, which proved to be the greatest technological advance since the introduction of rear-engined cars. [Source: In 1962, Lotus introduced a car with an aluminium monocoque chassis instead of the traditional tubular frame. question: What is the function of the virtual safety car in Formula 1?
last_answer: It is used to neutralise the race in cases where it is necessary to show a double yellow flag in some area of the track due to the existence of a risk for competitors or track marshals, without the incident requiring the appearance of the safety car. [Source: It is used to neutralise the race in cases where it is necessary to show a double yellow flag in some area of the track due to the existence of a risk for competitors or track marshals, without the incident requiring the appearance of the safety car.]   When the virtual safety car procedure is initiated, all luminous panels around the circuit will display the letters VSC and the teams will be notified. [Source: When race control initiates the virtual safety car procedure, all luminous panels around the circuit will display the letters VSC and the teams will be notified.]  Drivers must respect a minimum time established by the FIA at least once at each control post. [Source: Drivers must respect a minimum time established by the FIA at least once at each control post.]
